title: AWS Devops PRO Knowledge Test
description: Test your understanding of AWS Devops PRO with 25 questions.

questions.question: You're maintaining a small containerized REST API deployed to AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1, built from GitHub and deployed via a beginner CodePipeline. Add a security gate: automatically enable ECR image scanning on every build and block promotion if the image reports CVEs above a low threshold. Outline the approach, minimal CloudFormation, and tests?

options.text: Enable ECR image scanning on push and include a CodePipeline gate that runs a Lambda to parse image scan findings (VULNERABILITY_SUMMARY) and fail promotion if CVEs exceed threshold (e.g., CVEs > 2).

Propose a staged pipeline: source from Git to CodePipeline; CodeBuild runs unit tests and data quality checks against staging data in S3; Terraform plan/apply runs in a staging account; promote to pro

Implement SBOMs via CycloneDX in the build, produce SLSA provenance, sign artifacts with a KMS-backed key, and enforce with an OPA policy in the CD pipeline. On policy failure, halt deployment, rollba

Enable X-Ray tracing for API Gateway and Lambda (Mode: Active), add a CloudWatch dashboard tracking Latency and 5xx errors, and set alarms: average latency > 200ms or 5xx errors > 1 per minute for 5 m

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests practical security gate integration in a real pipeline, reinforcing basics of container scanning, Lambda gates, and basic CFN.

## Key Concepts

- CodePipeline gates and Lambda interactions

- End-to-end smoke testing

## Code Example
```javascript
// pseudo: Lambda reads ECR scan findings and fails if threshold exceeded
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you adapt for multiple thresholds per environment?
- What logging/alerting would you add to track failed gates?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design a serverless, multi-region DR with policy-as-code, traffic management, and chaos testing in a realistic production-like setup.

## Key Concepts

- Serverless, multi-region DR patterns
- DynamoDB Global Tables and cross-region replication semantics
- CDK multi-region deployments and artifacts
- Policy enforcement with OPA Gatekeeper
- DNS routing with Route 53 health-based routing
- Chaos engineering using AWS Fault Injection Simulator

## Code Example

```javascript
// CDK skeleton for multi-region deployment
const app = new App();
new Stack(app, 'IngestStack', { env: { region: 'us-east-1' }});
new Stack(app, 'IngestStackDR', { env: { region: 'us-west-2' }});
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you validate idempotent writes across regions?
- Which metrics and alarm strategies ensure DR readiness and quick rollback?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests practical blue/green deployment with ECS and CodeDeploy, enforcing safe rollout with alarms and rollback. Encourages precise CFN syntax and testing steps.

## Key Concepts
- ECS blue/green deployments
- CodeDeploy DeploymentGroup
- Traffic routing and TaskSets
- CloudWatch alarms and rollback
- CodePipeline integration

## Code Example
```yaml
# Minimal CloudFormation for CodeDeploy ECS blue/green
Resources:
  MyCodeDeployDeploymentGroup:
    Type: AWS::CodeDeploy::DeploymentGroup
    Properties:
      ApplicationName: !Ref MyCodeDeployApp
      DeploymentConfigName: CodeDeployDefault.ECSAllAtOnce
      ServiceRoleArn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole
      DeploymentStyle:
        DeploymentType: BLUE_GREEN
        DeploymentOption: WITH_TRAFFIC_CONTROL
      ECSService:
        ClusterName: !Ref MyCluster
        ServiceName: !Ref MyService
      TrafficRoutingConfig:
        Type: ECS
      BlueGreenDeploymentConfiguration:
        TerminateBlueInstancesOnDeploymentSuccess:
          Value: true
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test the rollback path in a staging environment?
- What metrics would you monitor during the 15-minute window?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to create ephemeral, per-PR test environments with minimal IaC, DNS wiring, and cleanup, all in a beginner-friendly way. It touches CloudFormation, ECS/Fargate, ALB, Route 53, and automation hooks.

## Key Concepts

- PR-based ephemeral environments
- CloudFormation templates with parameters
- ECS Fargate and ALB setup
- Route 53 alias records for preview DNS
- Automatic cleanup on PR close and health gates

## Code Example

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Parameters:
  PRTag:
    Type: String
  ContainerImage:
    Type: String
  DomainName:
    Type: String
Resources:
  PreviewALB:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub 'preview-alb-${PRTag}'
      Scheme: internet-facing
      Type: application
  PreviewTargetGroup:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup
    Properties:
      Port: 80
      Protocol: HTTP
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
  PreviewListener:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener
    Properties:
      LoadBalancerArn: !Ref PreviewALB
      Port: 80
      DefaultActions:
        - Type: forward
          TargetGroupArn: !Ref PreviewTargetGroup
  PreviewDNSRecord:
    Type: AWS::Route53::RecordSet
    Properties:
      HostedZoneName: !Sub '${DomainName}.'
      Name: !Sub 'preview-${PRTag}.${DomainName}'
      Type: A
      AliasTarget:
        DNSName: !GetAtt PreviewALB.DNSName
        HostedZoneId: !GetAtt PreviewALB.CanonicalHostedZoneID
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you tear down old preview environments safely to avoid resource leaks or rate limits?
- What IAM policies would you apply to the deployment role to enforce least privilege across per-PR stacks?

questions.question: You're deploying a REST API on AWS Lambda + API Gateway, sourced from GitHub and deployed via a beginner CodePipeline. Implement exactly-once processing using a DynamoDB deduplication table keyed by shard+sequence number, with Lambda writing to the regional S3 data lake using deterministic partitioning keys and Cross-Region Replication…

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests serverless deployment, feature flagging, and safe canary rollout basics in a beginner context.

## Key Concepts
- Lambda alias routing and incremental traffic shifting
- SSM Parameter Store feature flags
- CodePipeline integration for serverless apps
- CloudWatch Synthetics for smoke tests
- Simple rollback strategies

## Code Example
```yaml
Resources:
  CanaryAlias:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Alias
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref MyFunction
      FunctionVersion: !GetAtt MyFunction.Version
      Name: canary
      RoutingConfig:
        AdditionalVersionWeights:
          - Version: 1
            Weight: 0.1
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you revert a failed canary without impacting prod?
- What alerts would you configure in CloudWatch?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Assess cross-region DR for a stateful workload, ensuring data integrity, policy enforcement, and automated rollback under real latency and 5xx conditions.

## Key Concepts
- Aurora Global Database with cross-region replication
- FluxCD HelmRelease, IRSA, OPA Gatekeeper
- Route 53 latency-based routing and health checks

## Code Example
```yaml
apiVersion: helm.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: analytics-app
spec:
  chart:
    repository: https://charts.example.com
    name: analytics
  values:
    global:
      region: us-east-1
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test DR runbooks under simulated lag?
- How would you enforce drift detection across regions?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Assesses end-to-end CI/CD across accounts and regions, with concrete rollout, observability, and DR readiness.

## Key Concepts
- Cross-account, cross-region CI/CD using GitHub Actions and AWS CDK
- ECS/Fargate and Helm-based deployments
- Per-branch canaries with weighted App Mesh routes
- Route 53 latency-based routing and health checks
- SLO-driven rollback using OpenTelemetry
- DR runbook with cross-region failover and data replay
- Guardrails via IAM Roles Anywhere and OPA Gatekeeper

## Code Example
```javascript
// Example: CDK-like snippet (pseudo) for weighted routing
const route = appMesh.addWeightedRoute('CanaryRoute', {
  weightedTargets: [
    { virtualNode: canaryVn, weight: 5 },
    { virtualNode: prodVn, weight: 95 }
  ],
});
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you handle drift detection between desired and actual deployment states across accounts?
- What metrics and alerting would you add to detect degraded SLOs early and trigger rollback automatically?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Explores a safe, low-risk deployment pattern for serverless apps.

## Key Concepts
- Blue/Green deployments with Lambda
- CodeDeploy deployment group and aliases
- CloudFormation minimal changes and traffic shifting
- Basic testing and rollback criteria

## Code Example
```yaml
# Minimal CFN snippet for CodeDeploy deployment group
# This is a placeholder example to illustrate structure
Resources:
  MyDeploymentGroup:
    Type: AWS::CodeDeploy::DeploymentGroup
    Properties:
      ApplicationName: !Ref MyCodeDeployApp
      DeploymentConfigName: CodeDeployDefault.LambdaCanary10Percent5Minutes
      ServiceRoleArn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CodeDeployServiceRole
      DeploymentStyle:
        DeploymentType: BLUE_GREEN
        DeploymentOption: WITH_TRAFFIC_CONTROL
      TriggerConfigurations: []
      Aliases:
        - blue
        - green
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you monitor Canary traffic and automate rollback criteria?
- What are potential pitfalls with API Gateway stages during canary shifts?

Two identical stacks in us-east-1 and us-west-2. Deploy East via CodePipeline; replicate artifacts to West with S3 replication; Route 53 health-check failover routes traffic to the healthy region. Pro

Plan uses CDK to deploy dual-region stacks, Lambda alias-based blue/green, per-branch previews, AppConfig flags per env, and OpenTelemetry to validate SLIs. Rollback flips alias weights to prior versi

questions.explanation:
Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design DR for streaming analytics across regions with consistent data parity, policy enforcement, and clear runbooks.

## Key Concepts

- Cross-region DR, streaming pipelines
- Kinesis Data Streams, Kinesis Data Analytics
- Redshift pipelines, S3 replication
- Route 53 failover, alarms
- IAM Roles Anywhere, OPA/Gatekeeper

## Code Example

```yaml
# DR config sketch
regions: 
  - us-east-1
  - eu-west-1

dr:
  strategy: active-passive
  failover_trigger:
    - latency > 200ms
    - data_parity != true
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you validate SLI correctness under partial outages?
- How would you handle schema evolution in the streaming app across regions?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

This question evaluates practical expertise in multi-region cloud architecture, progressive delivery strategies, infrastructure drift management, and security best practices for enterprise-scale deployments.

## Key Concepts

- Cross-region infrastructure orchestration with Terraform workspaces
- Progressive delivery using Argo Rollouts canary analysis
- Dynamic configuration management with AWS AppConfig
- Security hardening through IAM Roles for Service Accounts (IRSA)
- Continuous compliance with Terraform drift detection
- Automated rollback policies based on SLO metrics
- Synthetic monitoring for cross-region health verification
- S3-backed artifact versioning for deployment traceability

## Code Example

```yaml
# Terraform workspace configuration
terraform {
  backend "s3" {
    bucket = "infra-state-store"
    key    = "eks-microservice/terraform.tfstate"
    region = "us-east-1"
  }
}

# Regional module instantiation
module "primary_region" {
  source          = "./modules/eks-region"
  region          = "us-east-1"
  app_config_name = var.app_config_primary
  environment     = var.environment
}

module "secondary_region" {
  source          = "./modules/eks-region"
  region          = "us-west-2"
  app_config_name = var.app_config_secondary
  environment     = var.environment
}
```

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Assesses real-world disaster recovery, multi-region CI/CD, and secure cross-account access patterns.

## Key Concepts
- Multi-region Lambda deployments with aliases
- API Gateway traffic shifting and canaries
- DynamoDB Global Tables for data replication
- Terraform for cross-account IaC and drift detection
- Secrets rotation and least-privilege IAM
- Observability and rollback strategies

## Code Example
```terraform
# Terraform example: cross-region alias for Lambda with canary shift
resource "aws_lambda_alias" "prod" {
  name             = "prod"
  function_name    = aws_lambda_function.api.arn
  function_version = "$LATEST"
  routing_config {
    additional_version_weights = {
      "2" = 0.1  # 10% canary traffic
    }
  }
}
```

questions.explanation:
Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to architect multi-region GitOps with policy enforcement and DR realism.

Key Concepts
- Argo CD and Helm deployments across clusters
- AWS IRSA for granular IAM
- OPA Gatekeeper for policy compliance
- Istio canaries and traffic shifting
- Route 53 latency routing with DNS failover
- DR runbooks, cross-account IAM design
- Cost governance with Budgets

Code Example
```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: streaming-app
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: 'git@github.com:org/streaming-app.git'
    path: 'k8s/production'
    targetRevision: HEAD
  destination:
    server: 'https://kubernetes.default.svc'
    namespace: streaming
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true
```

Follow-up Questions
- How would you validate data lag and 5xx-based rollbacks in DR?
- How would you scale per-account IAM roles and policy-as-code across regions?

Use FluxCD to deploy Helm charts, IRSA for service accounts, and OPA Gatekeeper for policy checks. ## Why This Is Asked

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests ability to design multi-region, GPU-backed inference with safe traffic shifting, policy enforcement, and chaos testing.

## Key Concepts
- Cross-region routing with Route 53 weighted records
- SageMaker GPU endpoints and warm standby strategies
- Per-tenant quotas via IAM Roles Anywhere and OPA Gatekeeper policies
- Chaos engineering with AWS FIS for GPU/node/network failures
- Observability with OpenTelemetry for SLA validation
- DR runbook including rollback criteria and post-mortem

## Code Example
```javascript
// Pseudo function to compute canary weight
function canaryWeight(total, canaryPct) {
  return Math.round(total * (canaryPct/100));
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you isolate tenant data during cross-region failover?
- How would you test rollback criteria without impacting live traffic?

- Least-privilege IAM roles with cross-account access using IRSA and resource-based policies

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked

Tests ability to design cross-region, multi-account CI/CD with feature flags, observability, and a formal DR plan.

## Key Concepts

- Multi-account, multi-region deployment
- Lambda aliases for blue/green rollout
- Per-branch preview environments
- AppConfig feature flags per environment
- OpenTelemetry for end-to-end SLIs across regions
- DR runbook with failover, rollback criteria, and IAM guardrails

## Code Example

```javascript
// CDK sketch: create a Lambda version and an alias for live traffic
const fn = new lambda.Function(this, 'ETL', { /*...*/ });
const v1 = new lambda.Version(this, 'ETLVer1', { lambda: fn });
const liveAlias = new lambda.Alias(this, 'ETLLive', {
  aliasName: 'live',
  version: v1,
  additionalVersions: [],
});
```

## Follow-up Questions

- How would you validate rollback integrity under simulated traffic?
- How do you handle stateful data consistency during cross-region failover?

## Why This Is Asked

    region = \"us-east-1\" 
  }
}
```

- What metrics and alerts indicate a failed rollback?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Demonstrates practical, beginner-friendly automation with GitHub Actions, OIDC, and AWS serverless deployment, plus rollback strategies.

## Key Concepts
- GitHub Actions with OIDC federation
- IAM role trust and least privilege
- CloudFormation for Lambda/API Gateway
- S3 as artifact store
- CFN rollback on failure and basic health checks

## Code Example
```javascript
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
permissions:
  id-token: write
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v2
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/GitHubActionsCFNRole
          aws-region: us-east-1
      - uses: aws-actions/cloudformation-deploy@v2
        with:
          stack-name: serverless-api
          template: template.yaml
          no-execute-changeset: true
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test failover and rollback in this pipeline?
- How to extend this with additional environments (dev/stage/prod)?

## Why This Is Asked
This tests practical, beginner-friendly capability to implement feature flags in a real pipeline with guardrails.

- Feature flags with SSM Parameter Store
- Lightweight CI/CD with CodePipeline
- Lambda-based deployment guards and basic rollback

# CloudFormation snippet highlights (pseudo)
Resources:
  FeatureFlagParam:
    Type: AWS::SSM::Parameter
    Properties:
      Name: /feature/newEndpoint
      Type: String
      Value: false
      Tier: Standard
```

```javascript
// Lambda guard sketch (pseudo)
exports.handler = async (event) => {
  // read CloudWatch metrics; if 5xx >1% for 2 checks, fail
};
```
```

- What metrics would you surface to optimize the flag rollout?

new ecs.FargateService(this, 'Svc', {
  cluster,
  taskImageOptions: { image: ecs.ContainerImage.fromAsset('path') },
  deploymentController: { type: ecs.DeploymentControllerType.CODE_DEPLOY }
});
```

- How would you rotate and audit secrets across regions?

questions.explanation:
## Why This Is Asked
Tests integration of Vault with Kubernetes across regions, focusing on secure dynamic secrets, TTL/renewals, and deployment safety in a real-world FluxCD setup.

## Key Concepts
- Vault dynamic secrets and Kubernetes auth
- Per-tenant roles and lease TTLs
- Vault Agent sidecars for secret injection
- Canary deployments and rollback triggers
- Observability and audit trails (CloudWatch, IAM trails)

## Code Example
```javascript
// Vault policy (example) - grants read to dynamic DB creds
path "database/creds/*" {
  capabilities = ["read"]
}
```

## Follow-up Questions
- How would you test rotation in a staging environment without impacting tenants?
- What metrics indicate a rotation caused a service issue and require automatic rollback?
